ABOUT ME

I'm Iryna — a 23-year-old media and business enthusiast who thrives at the intersection of research, innovation, and real-world impact. With 5+ projects across multiple countries, I turn complex challenges into clear strategies and compelling experiences.

At 23, I've already learned that the most interesting problems sit at the edges — where data meets storytelling, where strategy meets live experience, where research meets real people. That's where I work best.

Over the past few years I've contributed to 5+ projects spanning multiple countries, taking on roles as project manager, researcher, presenter, and strategist.

5+ Projects

PROJECTS

BUas × Dentsu Lab — Amsterdam

Spotje

Participated in a Blended Intensive Programme (BIP) in collaboration with Dentsu Lab, working within a diverse international student team over one week in Amsterdam. The project focused on analysing third spaces in the city and developing solutions to improve how people — particularly children and newcomers — engage with public urban environments. The team produced Spotje, a location-based exploration app concept, including user research, data analysis, and a full commercialisation strategy.

The Problem

By age 12, 98% of Dutch children use a phone daily. Screen time is rising, physical activity is dropping, and kids are increasingly alone — even when surrounded by others. Public spaces exist, but children have no compelling reason to use them.

The Idea

Spotje is a location-based quest app that turns the city into a playground. Children aged 7–12 follow city-curated routes, stopping at real landmarks to complete challenges — photographing spots, solving co-op puzzles, talking to strangers. The phone becomes the tool that gets them off the phone.

My Role & Contribution

As part of an 8-person international team, I contributed to the research foundation, user persona development, and commercialisation strategy — shaping how Spotje could grow from a free city app into a national programme integrated with schools, municipalities, and brand partners like Nike and Decathlon.

Skills

  • User Research & Persona Development
  • Commercialisation Strategy
  • Data Analysis of Urban Third Spaces
  • Brand Partnership Frameworks (Nike, Decathlon)
  • Go-to-Market Planning
24h Hackathon — St. Pölten × EUDRES

Shaping Regional Identities with EUDRES

A 24-hour hackathon held in St. Pölten, organized as part of the EUDRES program, bringing together international student teams to tackle one of the most pressing questions in AI adoption: how do we make human-AI collaboration actually work in high-stakes environments? Using the medical sector as a real-world lens, our team developed the RAD-Framework — a practical, regulation-grounded approach to deploying AI responsibly where the cost of getting it wrong is highest.

The Problem

AI moving faster than regulation; no universal competency standards; EU AI Act Article 4 compliance gap.

The Idea — RAD Framework

A 3-pillar cyclical system for responsible AI deployment in high-stakes environments:

1.AI Certification
2.Monitoring
3.Doctor Cooperation

My Role & Contribution

Problem framing, framework structure, pitch narrative, go-to-market strategy for Cloudflight.

What Was Built

Framework concept, certification model, and hackathon pitch delivered to industry judges.

Design Thinking Project

Radicalization through Social Media

How does extremist content reach children online — and what can we do about it? Through a design thinking process, our team of three researched how radicalisation quietly spreads through memes, algorithms, and short-form video, conducted empathy mapping and user interviews, and uncovered a key gap: parents are left in the dark while kids easily bypass existing safeguards.

Our answer was SafeScroll — an AI-powered app that filters harmful content in real time and gives parents clear, actionable visibility into what their child encounters online.

The Problem

Extremist and harmful content increasingly reaches children through algorithmic amplification on platforms like TikTok, Instagram, and YouTube — often disguised as humour and memes. Existing parental controls are easy to bypass, and reporting tools are confusing and ineffective.

The Idea

Design an AI-powered overlay app that scans social media content in real time, flags harmful posts, and provides parents with a transparent dashboard — while keeping the child's experience age-appropriate and educational rather than just restrictive.

What We Built

A dual-dashboard mobile app prototype featuring real-time AI content filtering (using BERT, CLIP, MobileNet, and OCR), categorised threat labelling (hate speech, manipulation, extremist content), adjustable filter strength, and separate parent and child views.

My Role & Contribution

I contributed to research design, empathy mapping, and the narrative arc of the presentation — translating complex findings about online radicalisation into a clear, human-centred story grounded in real user pain points. I also shaped the problem framing and helped structure the design thinking process from insight to concept.

Industry Partnership · Joyn

World Wide Wien

We had the opportunity to collaborate with Joyn — one of Germany's leading free streaming platforms — to develop a full expansion pitch for their original show World Wide Wien, hosted by the Wolter twins, Benni & Dennis. Our task was to take an already-loved entertainment format and ask: what happens when you bring it to Vienna? We developed the creative concept, episode structure, games, guest strategy, and interactivity model — then backed it all with a real business case, budget breakdown, revenue projections, and go-to-market plan.

The Problem

Working directly with Joyn, one of Germany's leading free streaming platforms, we had the chance to develop an original show concept built on a real question: how do you take an already-loved entertainment format and make it fresh, culturally specific, and scalable?

The Idea

Take the World Wide Wien format — comedy, celebrity guests, music, challenges — and reimagine it with an Austrian twist. Drop the Wolter twins into the heart of Vienna, put German and Austrian culture in playful competition, and let the audience pick a side. Austrian dialect rounds, Schnitzel-coating challenges, grumpy Viennese imitations, iconic locations like Schönbrunn and Stephansplatz.

My Role & Contribution

I shaped the creative concept and episode structure, designed the culturally-rooted games and challenges, contributed to the guest and interactivity strategy, and supported the business case to prove the show could actually work.
